project_summary:
Replaces the hardcoded `INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED` ES-module constant with a user-data-directory manifest file (`~/.aitri-hub/integration-compat.json`) that is re-read on every collection cycle and written only via an explicit `aitri-hub integration review <version>` CLI subcommand. The review command computes a SHA-256 over the relevant CHANGELOG.md section and stores it in the manifest as provenance, so clearing the integration warning requires the developer to actually touch the CHANGELOG entry — not merely edit a number.

Product Analysis:
  North Star KPI: Time-to-clear — developer can clear a fresh integration warning end-to-end (run command → dashboard updates) in ≤10 seconds with no process restart.
  JTBD: 'When a new Aitri CLI ships, let me record that I actually reviewed its integration impact, and stop nagging me — without editing source, rebuilding, or restarting the web server.'
  Guardrail metric: Manifest read must add ≤20ms to a single collection cycle measured on the existing NFR-001 20-project load.
no_go_zone: No automatic detection of changes in docs/integrations/CHANGELOG.md — the developer must explicitly run `aitri-hub integration review` to re-attest; there is no file watcher that auto-clears the alert
No network fetch of CHANGELOG.md in v1 — the command reads a local path supplied via argument or bundled with the Hub package; remote retrieval is out of scope

No retroactive migration — the existing hardcoded `INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED = '0.1.80'` is not auto-copied into the manifest at upgrade time; users opt in by running the review command (fallback path preserves today's behavior until they do)
No multi-user review flow — no signing, no peer approval, no review log beyond the single most recent attestation per installation; this is single-developer attestation only
No changes to how sub-feature pipelines (features/*/) consume the integration gate — they keep reading the aggregated dashboard.json output exactly as today
No redesign of the React IntegrationAlertBanner visual style — this feature only adds two provenance fields (reviewedAt, changelogHash) to the alert payload; UI refresh is deferred

user_personas.goal: Clear the Aitri integration alert immediately after upgrading the CLI, without stopping the running `aitri-hub web` process and without editing source files
user_personas.pain_point: Today the only way to clear the warning is to edit `lib/constants.js`, reinstall globally, and restart the web process — a 5-step workflow that is easy to skip or rubber-stamp

user_personas.goal: Ensure that every recorded review was grounded in an actual read of the CHANGELOG section, not a thoughtless version bump
user_personas.pain_point: The current constant-bump workflow provides zero evidence that a review happened; maintainers cannot distinguish a considered attestation from a `git blame one-liner`

user_personas.goal: See on the web dashboard whether the integration review is current and who/when attested it, without reading source files
user_personas.pain_point: The current alert is opaque — it says 'not reviewed past X' but gives no surface for when or by what mechanism the review could be updated

functional_requirements.title: Integration-compat manifest file schema and location
functional_requirements.description: Hub must read and write a manifest file at `${AITRI_HUB_DIR}/integration-compat.json` (default `~/.aitri-hub/integration-compat.json`). The file is a JSON document with the shape `{ schemaVersion: '1', reviewedUpTo: '<semver>' | null, reviewedAt: '<ISO-8601>' | null, changelogHash: '<64-hex>' | null, reviewerNote: '<string>' | null }`. When absent or malformed, the collector must treat the effective `reviewedUpTo` as the package-declared fallback baseline (FR-034) — it must not crash or block collection.
acceptance_criteria: Given no `integration-compat.json` file exists, when the collector runs, then the effective reviewedUpTo is the package fallback baseline and the dashboard's `integrationAlert` is computed accordingly without error

Given a well-formed manifest with `reviewedUpTo: '0.1.82'`, when the collector runs, then the integration-guard compares against '0.1.82' and suppresses the alert when the detected CLI equals that value

Given a manifest file whose JSON is malformed (truncated, invalid syntax), when the collector runs, then the collector logs a WARN to ~/.aitri-hub/logs/aitri-hub.log, treats the manifest as absent, and continues — no exception propagates to the collection loop

Given a manifest file whose `reviewedUpTo` is not a valid `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` string, when the collector runs, then the manifest is treated as absent with a WARN log; the collector must not silently accept the invalid value

Given the default data directory does not yet exist, when any consumer of the manifest reads it for the first time, then the read returns `absent` without creating the directory; the directory is created only on successful write by the review command

functional_requirements.title: `aitri-hub integration review <version>` CLI subcommand
functional_requirements.description: Add a new CLI subcommand `aitri-hub integration review <version> [--changelog <path>] [--note <string>]` that writes the manifest. When `--changelog` is omitted, the command resolves the path to the CHANGELOG.md bundled with the installed Aitri CLI (queried via `aitri --changelog-path` or a documented fallback). The command must refuse to write when the version cannot be located as a section heading in the CHANGELOG. On success, the command exits 0 and prints the written manifest path and reviewedUpTo value.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user runs `aitri-hub integration review 0.1.82` while a local CHANGELOG contains a `## 0.1.82` section, when the command runs, then a manifest file is written with reviewedUpTo:'0.1.82', reviewedAt is a valid ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, changelogHash is a 64-char lowercase hex string, and exit code is 0

Given the user runs `aitri-hub integration review 99.99.99` for a version not present in the resolved CHANGELOG, when the command runs, then nothing is written, stderr contains the substring 'changelog entry not found', and exit code is non-zero
Given the user runs `aitri-hub integration review` with no version argument, when the command runs, then stderr prints a usage line including 'version' and exit code is non-zero

Given a prior successful review for the same version, when the user runs the review command again for the same version, then the command is idempotent — it exits 0, the manifest's changelogHash matches the prior value, and reviewedAt is refreshed to the new timestamp (not a duplicate record)
Given the user passes `--note "ops-approved"`, when the command writes the manifest, then reviewerNote equals the exact string 'ops-approved' and round-trips through read without transformation

functional_requirements.title: CHANGELOG section detection and SHA-256 hashing
functional_requirements.description: The review command locates the CHANGELOG section for the requested version by matching a top-level markdown heading containing the version string (e.g. `## 0.1.82`, `## [0.1.82]`, `## 0.1.82 — YYYY-MM-DD`). The section body spans from that heading to the next top-level heading or end-of-file. The computed changelogHash is `sha256(utf8(sectionBody))` as lowercase hex. Leading and trailing whitespace in the captured body must be trimmed before hashing so trivial edits (newline additions at the end) do not alter the hash.
acceptance_criteria: Given a CHANGELOG with a heading `## 0.1.82 — 2026-04-20` followed by three paragraphs, when the section body is extracted, then the captured text starts at the line after the heading and ends at the character before the next `## ` heading
Given the same section hashed twice in succession, when sha256 is computed, then the two hashes are identical
Given a CHANGELOG section that is modified by inserting a single character, when the hash is recomputed, then the new hash differs from the previous hash
Given the requested version does not appear as a heading in the CHANGELOG, when the review command runs, then `changelog entry not found` is emitted to stderr and no hash is written
Given the section body contains a trailing blank line, when the hash is computed, then the result matches the hash computed without the trailing blank line (trim before hash)

functional_requirements.title: Collector re-reads manifest on every collection cycle without process restart
functional_requirements.description: The collector must call a read function that loads the manifest from disk on every invocation of `collectAll()` (i.e. every `REFRESH_MS` tick in `aitri-hub web`). The result must NOT be imported at module-load time and must NOT be cached across cycles inside the collector. After a successful `aitri-hub integration review` call, the next collection cycle must reflect the new state within `REFRESH_MS` milliseconds.
acceptance_criteria: Given a running `aitri-hub web` process with a warning alert active, when the user runs `aitri-hub integration review <currentVersion>` in a separate shell, then within one `REFRESH_MS` tick the new dashboard.json is written with `integrationAlert: null`

Given two collection cycles separated by an external edit to the manifest file, when the second cycle runs, then the second cycle's integration-guard call uses the edited manifest's reviewedUpTo (not the first cycle's)

Given the manifest is deleted between two collection cycles, when the second cycle runs, then the collector falls back to the package baseline and writes the corresponding alert state — no exception, no stale reference to the prior manifest

Given the integration-guard is imported by the collector, when the module's source is inspected, then it must not retain a top-level `const reviewedUpTo = ...` expression that caches the value at import time

functional_requirements.title: Package-declared fallback baseline when manifest is absent
functional_requirements.description: The Hub package must declare a fallback baseline version — the last version the Hub maintainers reviewed at release time — exposed as a single read-only export. When no manifest exists, the integration-guard uses this fallback. The fallback replaces the role previously played by `INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED` but it is no longer the sole source of truth: it is explicitly a default-for-unconfigured-installs.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Hub package is freshly installed with no manifest written, when the collector runs, then the alert is computed against the fallback baseline (matching the current pre-feature behavior of `INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED`)
Given a manifest with reviewedUpTo:'0.1.85' exists and the fallback is '0.1.80', when the collector runs, then the integration-guard uses '0.1.85' — the manifest takes precedence over the fallback
Given the Hub package source is inspected, when searching for the fallback definition, then exactly one named export declares the fallback value and no other file duplicates it

functional_requirements.title: Integration-alert payload includes review provenance
functional_requirements.description: When the integration-guard emits an alert (non-null), the alert object must include two new optional fields alongside the existing `severity`, `message`, and `changelogUrl`: `reviewedAt` (ISO-8601 string or null) and `changelogHash` (64-hex string or null). When no manifest exists, both fields are null. When a manifest exists but still triggers an alert (because detectedVersion > reviewedUpTo), the fields carry the manifest's values so the web UI can render 'last reviewed at X, hash Y'.
acceptance_criteria: Given a manifest with reviewedUpTo:'0.1.80', reviewedAt:'2026-04-18T12:00:00Z', changelogHash:'abc123...' and a detected CLI version '0.1.82', when the collector writes dashboard.json, then `integrationAlert.reviewedAt === '2026-04-18T12:00:00Z'` and `integrationAlert.changelogHash === 'abc123...'`

Given no manifest exists and a detected CLI version greater than the fallback baseline, when the collector writes dashboard.json, then `integrationAlert.reviewedAt === null` and `integrationAlert.changelogHash === null`
Given the integration-guard returns null (no alert), when dashboard.json is written, then `integrationAlert === null` at the top level — provenance fields are not emitted separately

Given the existing React IntegrationAlertBanner renders a payload with the two new fields, when the payload contains them, then rendering does not regress — the banner still displays the message and link exactly as before (additive change only)

functional_requirements.title: Detect CHANGELOG drift since last review
functional_requirements.description: On each collection cycle, when a manifest exists, the collector must re-hash the CHANGELOG section for `manifest.reviewedUpTo` and compare it to `manifest.changelogHash`. When the two hashes differ, the collector must emit a distinct alert severity `warning` with message `integration changelog modified since review` — even if the detected CLI version is not greater than reviewedUpTo. This protects against the CHANGELOG being edited after the review was recorded.
acceptance_criteria: Given a manifest with reviewedUpTo:'0.1.82' and changelogHash:'H1' and the CHANGELOG section for 0.1.82 currently hashes to H1, when the collector runs, then no drift alert is emitted

Given a manifest with changelogHash:'H1' and the CHANGELOG for the same version now hashes to H2 (different from H1), when the collector runs, then `integrationAlert.message` contains the substring 'changelog modified since review' and severity is 'warning'
Given a manifest with changelogHash:null (never reviewed via this feature), when the collector runs, then no drift check is performed and behavior falls back to version comparison only

Given the CHANGELOG file is absent (cannot be located via the same resolution used in FR-031), when the collector runs, then the drift check is skipped with a WARN log and the version comparison proceeds normally

user_stories.i_want: a single command that marks a new Aitri CLI version as integration-reviewed
user_stories.so_that: I can clear the warning banner immediately after upgrading without editing source or restarting the web process

acceptance_criteria.given: the running `aitri-hub web` process is showing the warning `Aitri 0.1.82 detected — Hub integration not reviewed past 0.1.80` and a local CHANGELOG has a `## 0.1.82` section
acceptance_criteria.when: I run `aitri-hub integration review 0.1.82` in a separate shell
acceptance_criteria.then: within one `REFRESH_MS` tick, the dashboard refreshes and `integrationAlert` becomes null — no process restart is needed

acceptance_criteria.given: I run the review command with a version that is not in the CHANGELOG
acceptance_criteria.when: the command attempts to write the manifest
acceptance_criteria.then: the command exits non-zero with stderr 'changelog entry not found' and the manifest is left unchanged

user_stories.i_want: Hub to detect when the CHANGELOG section has been edited after I attested to it
user_stories.so_that: I cannot be fooled by a later edit into thinking my original attestation still covers the current text

acceptance_criteria.given: I previously ran `aitri-hub integration review 0.1.82` and the CHANGELOG section for 0.1.82 has since been edited
acceptance_criteria.when: the collector runs its next cycle
acceptance_criteria.then: the dashboard shows an alert with severity 'warning' and message containing 'changelog modified since review'

user_stories.i_want: to see when and how the integration review was recorded
user_stories.so_that: I can judge whether the review is current or stale without reading source

acceptance_criteria.given: a manifest exists with reviewedAt and changelogHash populated
acceptance_criteria.when: I open the web dashboard and the integration banner is present
acceptance_criteria.then: the banner's payload contains the reviewedAt timestamp and changelogHash fields so the UI can render provenance alongside the warning

user_stories.i_want: Hub to read my integration-review state from a manifest file in my user data directory
user_stories.so_that: I can inspect and version-control it locally without touching the Hub package source

acceptance_criteria.then: the process does not crash; the collector runs with the fallback baseline and the dashboard renders normally

acceptance_criteria.given: I manually create a valid manifest with reviewedUpTo:'0.1.82'
acceptance_criteria.then: the integration-guard compares the detected CLI version against '0.1.82' using the value from my manifest

user_stories.i_want: the review command to record a cryptographic fingerprint of the exact CHANGELOG section I attested to
user_stories.so_that: later edits to that section cannot pass as covered by my original review

acceptance_criteria.given: I run the review command against a CHANGELOG section containing three paragraphs
acceptance_criteria.then: the manifest's changelogHash is the SHA-256 of the trimmed body of that section as a 64-character lowercase hex string

acceptance_criteria.given: two runs of the same review on an unchanged CHANGELOG
acceptance_criteria.when: I compare the two resulting hashes

user_stories.i_want: Hub to reflect a new review within seconds of my running the command
user_stories.so_that: I never have to kill and restart the `aitri-hub web` process to clear a warning

acceptance_criteria.given: the `aitri-hub web` process has been running since before I installed the new Aitri CLI
acceptance_criteria.when: I run `aitri-hub integration review <currentVersion>` and wait one REFRESH_MS tick
acceptance_criteria.then: the web dashboard's integrationAlert transitions to null on the next refresh with no process-level intervention

user_stories.i_want: Hub to work out-of-the-box with no manifest configured
user_stories.so_that: a first install matches today's experience and does not require a one-time setup ritual before the dashboard renders

acceptance_criteria.given: a freshly installed Hub with no `integration-compat.json`
acceptance_criteria.then: the integration alert behaves exactly as it does today against the package-declared fallback baseline — no prompt, no error, no empty state

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Manifest read plus optional CHANGELOG re-hash must add no more than 20ms to a single collection cycle measured on a 20-project workload
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: A microbenchmark running collectAll() with 20 projects and a present manifest (with drift check enabled) completes within 20ms more than the same run with the manifest removed — measured as median of 50 runs

non_functional_requirements.requirement: A malformed manifest or CHANGELOG must never crash the collector or the web server
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given deliberately corrupted inputs (truncated JSON manifest, CHANGELOG with no matching section, CHANGELOG file absent), when collectAll() runs, then no exception propagates beyond the collector; each failure mode produces exactly one WARN log entry and the cycle completes with the correct fallback behavior described in FR-030/FR-036

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Paths used by the review command and the manifest read path must be constrained to the user data directory and the resolved CHANGELOG location; symlink escapes must not allow reads outside those roots
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given a symlink inside ~/.aitri-hub/ pointing to /etc/passwd, when the collector attempts to read the manifest via that symlink, then the read is rejected (fs.realpathSync check) and a WARN log is emitted — no contents outside the data directory are read

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every manifest read outcome (absent, malformed, valid-no-drift, valid-with-drift) must be observable via a single log line per collection cycle at INFO level
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given 4 consecutive cycles covering each of the 4 outcomes, when the aitri-hub.log file is inspected, then exactly 4 INFO entries with a stable event key (e.g. 'integration-manifest:absent', 'integration-manifest:malformed', 'integration-manifest:ok', 'integration-manifest:drift') are present

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The review command's success output must fit in 80 columns and include the written manifest path, the reviewedUpTo value, and the 12-char prefix of the changelogHash for quick eyeballing
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Running the review command with a terminal width of 80 columns produces an output block where no single line exceeds 80 characters and the three fields (path, version, hash prefix) are each visible on their own line

constraints: Node.js ≥18 for CLI; Docker for web dashboard (inherited from parent project)
Zero npm runtime dependencies — CLI must use Node built-ins only (crypto.createHash, fs, path, child_process)
All persistent data written only under ${AITRI_HUB_DIR} (default ~/.aitri-hub/) — never into project directories
The fallback baseline (FR-034) must have exactly one source-of-truth definition in the Hub package

The existing `INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED` export must remain importable (as the fallback baseline's underlying value) until a future feature deprecates it explicitly — this feature does NOT remove it from `lib/constants.js`
technology_preferences: CLI: Node.js (ESM, zero dependencies) — same as parent project
Hashing: built-in `crypto.createHash('sha256')` — no external hashing libraries
JSON I/O: built-in `JSON.parse`/`JSON.stringify` with atomic write via temp-file + rename — reuse the pattern established in `lib/store/dashboard.js`
CLI argument parsing: inline parser in `bin/aitri-hub.js` — same style as existing subcommands; no argparse library
